title: Never forget you can do whatever the fuck you want — 14-line Shanghai manifesto
html:
Never forget you can do whatever the fuck you want — 14-line Shanghai manifesto

Source: 2024-09-12 Apple Note. Written ~1 week into the Shanghai semester. The most concentrated first-principles self-talk doc in the corpus — 14 imperatives Yahir wrote for himself to read back later.

TL;DR

  • "Blazing your own path inspires others to do the same"
  • "God favors those that max effort commit" — recurring max effort commit doctrine.
  • "The energy you put out into the universe comes back, often in multiplicative ways"
  • "Walk as if god sent you, because he did" — pre-cursor to the recurring soul as beacon / "walk with God's eyes" line in 2024 12 14 departing china and the March 2025 post.
  • "The internal flame within you is always alive, be with people and do things that make it rage out of control, avoid people that dwindle the flame" — the flame metric for relationships and contexts, recurring throughout.
  • "You are going to die soon. Your life is like a rainbow; you appear, have a chance to blaze in the sky, then disappear." — direct echo of the Sahil Bloom-derived rainbow framing from 2023 12 29 vision for big life.
  • "Don't be afraid to peer into the void, don't get devoured by it" — ladders into "fall backwards into the void with a smile" from 2026.
  • "When faced with a difficult decision think: if I were to die tomorrow would I want to do this or that" — the memento-mori decision filter.
  • "Don't seek revenge, the universe handles karmic balance"

Pattern observed

This is a master template for Yahir's manifesto-style notes — short, second-person imperatives, distilled from a moment of clarity. The same voice + structure shows up later in 2024 10 30 thoughts on my future, 2025 03 15 than usual recently, 2026 02 26 fall backwards into void. He writes these to read again later, not to publish.

The flame/rainbow/void/walk-with-god vocabulary defined here becomes his stable spiritual lexicon for the next 18 months.

Open questions

  • What was the trigger? Lonely Shanghai night? Specific event?
  • Does he actually re-read these or are they write-once-and-forget?

Related

  • shanghai study abroad
  • max effort commit
  • soul as beacon
  • memento mori decision filter
  • 2023 12 29 vision for big life
  • 2024 12 14 departing china
  • 2026 02 26 fall backwards into void
  • china chapter
